description: Manually defined OpenAPI spec for endpoints being migrated to spec-first approach

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the API key. This helps identify the key's purpose

params.name: public_key
params.description: RSA public key in PEM format. This will be used to verify signatures on API requests

params.name: scopes
params.description: List of scopes to grant to the API key. Valid values are 'read' and 'write'. If 'write' is included, 'read' must also be included. Defaults to full access (['read', 'write']) if not provided.

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key ID to delete

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the API key. This helps identify the key's purpose

params.name: scopes
params.description: List of scopes to grant to the API key. Valid values are 'read' and 'write'. If 'write' is included, 'read' must also be included. Defaults to full access (['read', 'write']) if not provided.

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

params.name: accepted_side
params.description: The side of the quote to accept (yes or no)

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

params.name: no_bid
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: rest_remainder
params.description: Whether to rest the remainder of the quote after execution

params.name: rfq_id
params.description: The ID of the RFQ to quote on

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Optional subaccount number to place the quote under (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.name: yes_bid
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: contracts
params.description: The number of contracts for the RFQ. Whole contracts only. Contracts may be provided via contracts or contracts_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: contracts_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: market_ticker
params.description: The ticker of the market for which to create an RFQ

params.name: replace_existing
params.description: Whether to delete existing RFQs as part of this RFQ's creation

params.name: rest_remainder
params.description: Whether to rest the remainder of the RFQ after execution

params.name: subaccount
params.description: The subaccount number to create the RFQ for (direct members only; 0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.name: subtrader_id
params.description: The subtrader to create the RFQ for (FCM members only)

params.name: target_cost_centi_cents
params.description: DEPRECATED: The target cost for the RFQ in centi-cents. Use target_cost_dollars instead.

params.name: target_cost_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.description: Quote ID

params.description: RFQ ID

params.name: cursor
params.description: Parameter to specify the pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results. Leave empty for the first page.

params.name: with_nested_markets
params.description: Parameter to specify if nested markets should be included in the response. When true, each event will include a 'markets' field containing a list of Market objects associated with that event. Historical markets settled before the historical cutoff will not be included.

params.name: with_milestones
params.description: If true, includes related milestones as a field alongside events.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by event status. Possible values are 'unopened', 'open', 'closed', 'settled'. Leave empty to return events with any status.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Filter by series ticker

params.name: min_close_ts
params.description: Filter events with at least one market with close timestamp greater than this Unix timestamp (in seconds).

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Filter events with metadata updated after this Unix timestamp (in seconds). Use this to efficiently poll for changes.

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker

params.name: with_nested_markets
params.description: If true, markets are included within the event object. If false (default), markets are returned as a separate top-level field in the response. Historical markets settled before the historical cutoff will not be included.

params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 200.

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp in Unix seconds

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Candlestick period interval in minutes

params.name: include_latest_before_start
params.description:
If true, prepends the latest candlestick available before the start_ts. This synthetic candlestick is created by:
1. Finding the most recent real candlestick before start_ts
2. Projecting it forward to the first period boundary (calculated as the next period interval after start_ts)
3. Setting all OHLC prices to null, and `previous_price` to the close price from the real candlestick

params.description: Order ID

params.description: Action of the order

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: The original client-specified order ID to be amended

params.name: count
params.description: Updated quantity for the order (whole contracts only). If updating quantity, provide count or count_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: count_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: no_price
params.description: Updated no price for the order in cents

params.name: no_price_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.description: Side of the order

params.description: Optional subaccount number to use for this amendment (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.description: Market ticker

params.description: The new client-specified order ID after amendment

params.description: Updated yes price for the order in cents

params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: contracts_limit_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: reduce_by_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: reduce_to
params.description: Number of contracts to reduce to (whole contracts only). Reduce-to may be provided via reduce_to or reduce_to_fp; if both provided they must match. Exactly one of reduce_by(/reduce_by_fp) or reduce_to(/reduce_to_fp) must be provided.

params.name: reduce_to_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results. Leave empty for the first page.

params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results. Leave empty for the first page.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: contracts_limit
params.description: New maximum number of contracts that can be matched within this group over a rolling 15-second window. Whole contracts only. Provide contracts_limit or contracts_limit_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: contracts_limit_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., "10.00"; referred to as "fp" in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., "10", "10.0", "10.00"); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., "2.50") are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: enabled
params.description: Whether netting is enabled for this subaccount.

params.name: subaccount_number
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts).

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The ticker of the series to retrieve

params.name: include_volume
params.description: If true, includes the total volume traded across all events in this series.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Series ticker

params.name: show_historical
params.description: Show historical

params.name: category
params.description: Category

params.name: tags
params.description: Tags

params.name: include_product_metadata
params.description: Include product metadata

params.name: include_volume
params.description: If true, includes the total volume traded across all events in each series.

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Filter series with metadata updated after this Unix timestamp (in seconds). Use this to efficiently poll for changes.

params.description: The event ticker

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The series ticker

params.name: percentiles
params.description: Array of percentile values to retrieve (0-10000, max 10 values)

params.description: Start timestamp for the range

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp for the range

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Specifies the length of each forecast period, in minutes. 0 for 5-second intervals, or 1, 60, or 1440 for minute-based intervals.

params.name: ticker
params.description: The event ticker

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The series ticker

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp for the range

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp for the range

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Specifies the length of each candlestick period, in minutes. Must be one minute, one hour, or one day.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Series ticker - the series that contains the target market

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker - unique identifier for the specific market

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or after this time.

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or before this time.

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Time period length of each candlestick in minutes. Valid values are 1 (1 minute), 60 (1 hour), or 1440 (1 day).

params.name: include_latest_before_start
params.description:
If true, prepends the latest candlestick available before the start_ts. This synthetic candlestick is created by:
1. Finding the most recent real candlestick before start_ts
2. Projecting it forward to the first period boundary (calculated as the next period interval after start_ts)
3. Setting all OHLC prices to null, and `previous_price` to the close price from the real candlestick

params.name: ids
params.description: Filter by specific structured target IDs. Pass multiple IDs by repeating the parameter (e.g. `?ids=uuid1&ids=uuid2`).

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by structured target type

params.name: competition
params.description: Filter by competition. Matches against the league, conference, division, or tour in the structured target details.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of items per page (min 1, max 2000, default 100)

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor

params.name: structured_target_id
params.description: Structured target ID
